# Sagyboar

> Sagyboar is a self-hostable Platform as a Service (PaaS) that simplifies the deployment and management of applications and databases. The core product is open source and free to self-host, making it a compelling alternative to platforms like Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify. It is built on Docker and Traefik, and is available as a self-hosted installation or as a managed cloud product. Enterprise features—including SSO, fine-grained RBAC, audit logs, and white labelling—require a commercial licence. Sagyboar is designed for individual developers, small teams, and large enterprises alike.

Sagyboar lets teams deploy any application—using Nixpacks, Heroku Buildpacks, or a custom Dockerfile—alongside databases and Docker Compose stacks, across single or multiple servers. It supports Docker Swarm for clustering, real-time monitoring, automated backups, and granular role-based access control. AI-assisted deployments are supported via an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, enabling AI agents and tools to interact with Sagyboar directly.

Key facts:
- Core product is open source (GitHub: github.com/Sagyboar/Sagyboar) with 26,000+ stars and 200+ contributors; enterprise features require a commercial licence
- Over 6 million DockerHub downloads
- Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MariaDB, and Redis with built-in backup management
- Multi-server and Docker Swarm cluster support with zero-configuration setup
- Enterprise features include SSO/SAML (Okta, Azure), fine-grained RBAC, audit logs, and white labelling
- One-click templates for popular tools including Supabase, Cal.com, and PocketBase
- Full REST API, CLI access, and Swagger API reference
- Notifications via Slack, Discord, Telegram, email, and webhooks

## Product pages

- [Home](https://Sagyboar.com/): Product overview
- [Application Deployment](https://Sagyboar.com/features/application-deployment): Deploy any stack from any source in minutes
- [CI/CD & Git Integration](https://Sagyboar.com/features/ci-cd-git-integration): Automated pipelines from commit to production
- [AI Monitoring & Observability](https://Sagyboar.com/features/ai-monitoring-observability): Active intelligence, not passive dashboards
- [AI Error Diagnosis](https://Sagyboar.com/features/ai-error-diagnosis): From detection to assigned ticket in seconds
- [Autonomous Self-Healing](https://Sagyboar.com/features/autonomous-self-healing): Automated remediation with human oversight
- [Database Management](https://Sagyboar.com/features/database-management): Provision, back up, and restore databases
- [Multi-Server & Infrastructure](https://Sagyboar.com/features/multi-server-infrastructure): Centralized control over distributed infrastructure
- [Notifications & Alerting](https://Sagyboar.com/features/notifications-alerting): AI-enriched alerts where your team works
- [Managed Team Services](https://Sagyboar.com/features/managed-team-services): A dedicated team to keep your product running
- [Weekly Code Review](https://Sagyboar.com/features/weekly-code-review): AI-powered code health checks on your repositories
- [Security & Access Control](https://Sagyboar.com/features/platform-security-access-control): Enterprise-grade RBAC, SSO, and audit logs
- [Enterprise](https://Sagyboar.com/solutions/enterprise): Enterprise-grade features and support

## Pricing

- [Pricing](https://Sagyboar.com/pricing): Hobby ($4.50/mo per server), Startup (from $15/mo), Enterprise (contact sales), and Agency plans. The core product is open source and free to self-host; enterprise features (SSO, fine-grained RBAC, audit logs, white labelling) require a commercial licence.

## Compare & learn

- [Sagyboar vs Portainer](https://Sagyboar.com/Sagyboar-vs-portainer)
- [Sagyboar vs CapRover](https://Sagyboar.com/Sagyboar-vs-caprover)
- [Sagyboar vs Dokku](https://Sagyboar.com/Sagyboar-vs-dokku)
